title: Version documentation alongside the code
description: Tie each doc set to a release so readers on an older version see matching docs and breaking changes are isolated per version.
body: Use when documenting an API or product that ships multiple supported versions. Do not keep a single 'latest' page that silently mixes behavior from several releases. Actions: snapshot docs per major/minor, label the version prominently, keep a migration guide between versions, and link unreleased changes to a next/dev channel. Validation signals include a doc build per version and links resolving within the same version. Risk boundary: serving current docs to users on an old release causes broken examples and support load; record which version a page describes and gate changes behind the right version branch.
